journey.modeBeginnerSub: A lighter mode — write what you feel
journey.finishSwallow: Flavor lingered for {{sec}}s after the swallow.
journey.rotundoneBody: Sesquiterpene · perception threshold 16 ng/L. A black-pepper impression — the marker of Syrah / Grüner Veltliner.

openingStops.label: T0
openingStops.body: Reductive notes; the nose is closed. The first sip is firm and cold.

openingStops.label: 15m
openingStops.body: Dark-fruit notes stir faintly. Decanted into the carafe.

openingStops.label: 30m
openingStops.body: Violet unfurls and the nose begins to open.

openingStops.label: 1h
openingStops.body: Black pepper joins the cherry. A Rotundone marker.

openingStops.label: 2h
openingStops.body: Cigar box emerges; the tannins soften.

openingStops.label: 4h
openingStops.body: Aroma and structure integrate. Firm tannin resolves to fine-grained. The peak.

openingStops.label: 6h+
openingStops.body: Slowly descends into tertiary notes of mushroom and leather.

journey.verdictBandBody:
{{vintage}} · ideal at 13–30°C. {{peak}}.
With a wide, gentle range, it drinks well whenever you reach for it.
journey.closingFallback: Rewarding the long decant, this wine reached its peak in the fourth hour. The black-pepper impression lingers, and the fine-grained tannin calls for the next glass. I'd like to meet the next bottle after two or three more years of rest.

noteShare.bodyPlaceholder: Share your thoughts on this note...

body.pommard: Balloon decanter + 2 hours is standard for Les Rugiens. Reduction dissipating at 30 min is typical young Burgundy behavior.

p3.body: What struck me was the idea that the soil matters more than the label. "The same vineyard — the top five rows and the bottom five rows are different wines," she said.
p3.quote: "Wine is the farmer's time, not the winemaker's."
p3.body2: On the flight back I kept turning the phrase over. How different is the score we assign from the meaning the farmer saw in that year?
